page: Microsoft Azure D64ls_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dlsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The system architecture is x86_64, supported by a substantial L3 cache of 480 MB, alongside 48 KB L1d and 2 MB L2 caches. Memory capacity is constrained to 128.0 GB, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, though it is premium storage capable.

In benchmark evaluations, the D64ls_v7 demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities and high instruction throughput. The Passmark CPU score reaches a top-tier rating of 87182.31, supported by strong performance in extended instructions and encryption tests. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks are highly efficient, with gzip compression reaching 35.77 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.77 GB/sec, both placing in the top tier. Multi-core stress-ng performance is strong at 68669.54, while memory bandwidth remains robust, measuring 326.02 GB/sec for large-block read operations.

The primary architectural tradeoff of the D64ls_v7 is its low memory density of 2.0 GB per vCPU and the complete absence of local storage. This resource allocation profile makes it highly cost-efficient for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive in-memory databases or local scratch space. It is well-suited for tasks such as static web serving, where it achieves a top-tier throughput of 89.08 GB/sec, as well as multi-threaded decompression, cryptography, and CPU-based LLM prompt processing.
description: The Microsoft Azure D64ls_v7 is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dlsv7 family, powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs (32 physical cores) and 128.0 GB of RAM, representing a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports premium external storage. Benchmarks highlight top-tier performance in single-threaded gzip compression (35.77 GB/sec) and static web serving throughput (89.08 GB/sec), alongside strong multi-core CPU capabilities (Passmark CPU score of 87182.31). This configuration presents a clear tradeoff, prioritizing raw compute density and high clock speeds over memory capacity. It is highly suited for compute-intensive workloads such as static web serving, cryptography, multi-threaded decompression, and CPU-based LLM prompt processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64ls_v7 is a compute-optimized VM featuring 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs at 4.2 GHz, 128 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering top-tier web serving and compression performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64ls_v7 is a compute-optimized VM with 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and top-tier web serving and compression performance.
tagline: A compute-dense virtual machine featuring sixty-four dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and a low memory-to-core ratio for processing-heavy workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs running at 4.2 GHz with 480 MB L3 cache
128.0 GB system memory providing a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU
Top-tier single-threaded gzip compression and static web serving throughput
No local storage or bundled GPUs, requiring external premium storage
Optimized for compute-heavy tasks, cryptography, and multi-threaded decompression
